commit.message:
fix: use MYSQL_PWD env var instead of .cnf file for MySQL passwords

MySQL .cnf files don't properly handle passwords with % characters
even when quoted, as % is treated as a variable placeholder.
Using MYSQL_PWD environment variable instead, which correctly
handles all special characters while still keeping the password
out of process args (ps aux).

Fixes: #issue-with-special-chars-in-mysql-passwords

feat: StepScratchpad, no-progress detector, cache invalidation

Critical agent reliability fixes:

A. StepScratchpad — rolling context that tracks all step outcomes.
   Injected into self_heal() and _generate_edit() so the LLM knows
   what agent already accomplished/failed. Max 20 entries FIFO.

B. No-progress detector — if error count doesn't decrease after a
   heal iteration, abort early instead of wasting the entire budget
   repeating the same failing strategies.

C. File cache invalidation — after successful edit_file, the new
   content is written to _step_file_cache so subsequent edits to the
   same file see the current state (fixes 'text not found' on already-
   modified files).

Also enhanced _HEAL_SYSTEM prompt with self-correction directives:
- Re-read modified files before re-editing
- Never repeat failed edits
- Try completely different strategy after 2 failures

🔧 Fix: Add automatic retry logic for database connections

PROBLEM:
- InterfaceError: connection already closed still occurring
- Happens when Streamlit reruns and connection is closed
- Between page transitions, connections could be terminated

SOLUTION:
- Added execute_query_safe() wrapper function
- Automatic retry (2 attempts) on connection failure
- Handles both InterfaceError and OperationalError
- Connection is opened and closed within function scope
- Guarantees that connection is fresh for each query

BENEFITS:
- Resilient to server closing old connections
- Works reliably with cloud/remote databases
- User sees data even if first attempt fails
- Automatic recovery without manual intervention

* fix(combat): read mech extraCritCount from .MEC file for correct Cmd72 crit bytes

Root cause of 'Invalid Solaris COMBAT command: 13 not handled':
- Combat_ReadLocalActorMechState_v123 (@ 0x004456c0) reads
  extraCritCount+21 crit bytes from the Cmd72 packet, where extraCritCount
  is *(int16*)(mechData+0x3c) decoded from the mech's .MEC file.
- Server always sent 21 crit bytes (extraCritCount=0). For ANH-1A
  (FALLBACK_MECH_ID), extraCritCount=10, so client read 31 bytes,
  consuming 10 extra bytes from what followed, misaligning the remaining
  packet. The dispatch loop then saw 0x2E ('.' = command 13) as a CMD byte.

Fix:
- Add extraCritCount field to MechEntry (protocol/game.ts).
- Implement .MEC decryption in mechs.ts: sliding 4-byte XOR cipher seeded
  from the last 4 chars of the lowercase mech name (FUN_004427f0 +
  FUN_004428a0 + FUN_00442870 confirmed by Ghidra RE).
- Load extraCritCount from each .MEC file at startup in loadMechs().
- Add WORLD_MECH_BY_ID map for O(1) lookup.
- Pass correct critStateExtraCount and appropriately sized
  criticalStateBytes to buildCmd72LocalBootstrapPacket.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(combat): correct cmdIndex offset bug — tableIndex = ResearchCmd, not ResearchCmd+4

All buildGamePacket calls in combat.ts used ResearchCmd+4 as the table index
(e.g. Cmd72 → index 76, Cmd65 → index 69).  The dispatch table actually maps
ResearchCmd N directly to table index N.  Using +4 sent each command to the
wrong handler.

Root cause of 'Invalid Solaris COMBAT command: 13 not handled':
  - Cmd72 was sent with tableIndex=76 (wire 0x6d)
  - The handler at table[76] (FUN_0043D920) consumes no parse-buffer bytes
  - The dispatch loop then read the Cmd72 length-header byte 0x2E as the next
    command byte (0x2E − 0x21 = 13), finding no handler → fatal error

Fix: all 10 buildGamePacket calls shifted down by 4:
  Cmd64→64 (wire 0x61), Cmd65→65 (0x62), ..., Cmd72→72 (0x69), Cmd73→73 (0x6a)

Also fix:
  - Header comment: wire_byte = ResearchCmd + 0x21 (was +0x25)
  - COORD_BIAS comment: 26,100,312 (was 26,099,288)
  - All section-header wire/tableIndex annotations updated to match

* fix(M5): assign exits by cardinal quadrant to prevent oscillation

Previously getSolarisRoomExits sorted all rooms by distance and took the
4 nearest. This caused oscillation: from room B the room you just came
from (A) could land in the same slot as east, so clicking east again
sent you back to A.

Fixed by assigning one nearest room per N/E/S/W quadrant (using the
dominant axis of the dx/dy vector). The origin room now always ends up
in the opposite slot, so directional travel is consistent.

* fix(M5): correct compass slot axis assignment (Y=E/W, X=N/S)

Previous code treated dx as E/W and dy as N/S, which is opposite to
the actual SOLARIS.MAP coordinate orientation.  Confirmed from in-game
observation:
  - Starport(30,311) -> Int. Sector(42,388): dy dominates (+77), East btn
  - Int. Sector -> Chancellor's(196,366): dx dominates (+154), South btn

Correct mapping:
  slot 0 = North  (dx<0 dominates)
  slot 1 = South  (dx>0 dominates)
  slot 2 = East   (dy>0 dominates)
  slot 3 = West   (dy<0 dominates)

After this fix, travelling east plants the origin in the west slot.

* fix(M5): use provisional topology for all rooms; SOLARIS.MAP is visual-only

SOLARIS.MAP stores pixel positions on the travel-map bitmap image and
room descriptions.  It does NOT contain a room connection/adjacency
graph.  IS.MAP is the same format for IS-level planet positions.

The spatial adjacency heuristics added in the previous two commits were
based on a false assumption and produced wrong/oscillating results.

Remove all spatial inference from getSolarisRoomExits.  The function
now always uses the provisional linear topology (same as the pre-SOLARIS.MAP
fallback) for all rooms, regardless of whether the file was loaded.
SOLARIS.MAP is still loaded and used for room names, descriptions,
scene-slot indices, and the Cmd43 visual map display.

Update WorldRoom JSDoc to clarify centreX/Y are visual-only bitmap
coordinates.

commit.message:
🔧 Fix: Remove connection caching to prevent 'already closed' errors

PROBLEM:
- @st.cache_resource was keeping database connection in cache
- Between Streamlit reruns, server would close the connection
- Next query would fail with 'InterfaceError: connection already closed'

SOLUTION:
- Removed @st.cache_resource from get_db_conn()
- Each data load now creates a fresh connection
- Lightweight and reliable - PostgreSQL handles connection pooling

BENEFITS:
- No stale connections
- Works better with cloud database (remote server can close old conns)
- Each query gets a fresh, clean connection
- Faster failure detection with connect_timeout=5

This is the correct pattern for Streamlit + PostgreSQL.

Fix tag filter pattern in release.yml: glob syntax, not regex (#31)

`release.yml` uses `[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+*` as the tag filter, but GitHub
Actions tag filters use glob/fnmatch — not regex. In glob, `+` is a
literal character, so this pattern matches tags like `1+.2+.3+`, never
standard semver tags like `1.2.3`.

```yaml
# Before — + is literal in glob, never matches semver tags
tags:
  - '[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+*'

# After — * is glob wildcard, correctly matches 1.2.3 etc.
tags:
  - '[0-9]*.[0-9]*.[0-9]*'
```

fix: use force_update on seek to fix frontend progress sync

The MA server filters elapsed_time-only changes and sends a
lightweight QUEUE_TIME_UPDATED event instead of a full PLAYER_UPDATED.
The frontend does not always pick up these lightweight events for
PluginSource players, so the progress bar stays stale until page reload.

Pass force_update=True on significant changes (seek, track change) so
the server broadcasts a full PLAYER_UPDATED event that the frontend
handles correctly.  Regular throttled updates continue without
force_update to avoid unnecessary event bus traffic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: document technical report maintenance rules

Add detailed AGENTS guidance for future technical report updates so
later revisions preserve document-wide consistency instead of only
fixing local sections.

Document the key TR maintenance rules:
- American English across report, docs, and code-facing prose
- one-decimal percentage rounding with explicit exceptions for cited
  literature values and verbatim logs
- no space-separated thousands
- explicit CPU/GPU qualification for timing claims
- propagation of result changes through the narrative spine, tables,
  highlights, captions, cross-references, and title
- correct placement of implementation details vs benchmarks vs
  discussion content
- synchronization between the TR, figure generator, and current code
  defaults/capabilities

Also update AGENTS architecture wording to avoid overstating the method
as purely random where encoders may be data-adapted, and refresh the
streaming accuracy example to the rounded report value.

* Replace static builtInCommandHandler with AssignTaskCommandHandlerFunction class

Extract AssignTask handling into a dedicated CommandHandlerFunction
implementation modeled after AgenticCommandHandlerFunctionAdapter.
The new class holds its own produced/error event types and passes
them to AgentProcessSingleTickRunner.tick(), fixing the empty
registeredEventTypes bug. Remove builtInCommandHandler,
handleAssignTask, and resolveAgentByName statics from
KafkaAgenticAggregateRuntime. Fix missing ArrayList import in
ResumeNextAgentTaskTest.

* Remove single tick from all handlers that create AgentProcess

Remove the kick-start tick from AssignTaskCommandHandlerFunction,
AgenticCommandHandlerFunctionAdapter, and AgenticEventHandlerFunctionAdapter.
All AgentProcess advancement is now exclusively driven by the partition's
idle-poll cycle (resumeAgentTasks). Handlers only create the process and
return immediately (AssignTaskCommandHandlerFunction returns just the
AgentTaskAssignedEvent; the adapters return empty streams).

Removed getAllRegisteredEventTypes() from all three handlers since it was
only used for the tick call. Updated all corresponding tests to remove
Blackboard and getStatus mock setup.

commit.message:
fix: inject tool name after && / || for multi-step commands (#94)

* fix: inject tool name after && and || in multi-step commands (issue #87)

When the LLM generates multi-step commands like `sort ... && index ...`
instead of `sort ... && samtools index ...`, sanitize_args() now detects
the missing tool name and injects it automatically.

- Make `is_script_executable` pub(crate) so llm.rs can reuse it
- Extend `sanitize_args` to walk args and insert the tool name after
  each && / || token when the following token is not already the tool
  name, not a companion binary, and not a script executable
- Add 6 new unit tests covering: basic injection, already-correct
  output, || operator, companion binary preservation, three-step chains
